mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling, the discount rate on equities declines and risk appetite improves, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is falsified if rate cuts do not precede positive forward returns or if rising FEDFUNDS is associated with equal or better 20-day performance.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations with declining FEDFUNDS do not produce higher median 20-day SPX returns than rising or flat FEDFUNDS observations, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS level above 4.5% indicates restrictive policy that compresses valuation multiples and tightens financial conditions, creating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should weaken once policy stops rising.
failure_condition: Rejected if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% do not show lower median 20-day SPX returns than periods below the threshold, or if the threshold signal is positive in more than 40% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS implies weakening household income and deteriorating demand, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is strongest when unemployment rises while payroll growth is still decelerating.
failure_condition: Rejected if joint deterioration in UNRATE and PAYEMS does not predict lower 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the interaction term fails to outperform single-variable benchmarks in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in RSAFS signals weakening nominal consumption and revenue growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be visible when sales momentum turns down from recent highs.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls for 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Rejected if two-month sales declines are followed by positive median 20-day SPX returns, or if the triggered subset has no worse performance than all non-triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and cyclical acceleration, which should support earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when industrial production growth is broad-based rather than a one-month noise spike.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 3 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Rejected if three-month INDPRO upswings do not precede above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the triggered cases underperform the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in HOUST and RSAFS identifies a broad domestic demand rebound: housing activity improves income expectations while retail sales confirm consumption strength. Together they should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days, more strongly than either series alone.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint HOUST-RSAFS signal does not outperform single-series signals in predicting 60-day SPX returns, or if positive returns are not more frequent when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is near its cycle trough and payroll growth remains firm, equity markets should continue to price resilient earnings and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. This short-horizon effect should fade once unemployment starts rising.
failure_condition: Rejected if low-unemployment plus payroll growth does not yield higher 5-day SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if the signal breaks down once UNRATE starts rising.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflation phase, a falling CPIAUCSL combined with stable or lower FEDFUNDS signals improving real policy conditions, easing discount-rate pressure and supporting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails if inflation re-accelerates while rates stay high.
failure_condition: Rejected if declining CPIAUCSL with stable or falling FEDFUNDS does not predict above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward tests.
